memoryArtifacts.headline: Richard Cater III Land Grant of 1694 for Town of Dover
memoryArtifacts.description:
ew Hampshire, Town Clerk Vital and Town Records, 1636-1947
Dover Town Records, Vol. 3, p. 20) Image 14 of 253

On 11 April 1694 Richard III was granted land adjoining his father's plantation:
 	Aprill 11th 1694
By ye Comitte Chosen By ye ffreeholders of ye Towne of Dover for granting Lands given and granted unto Richard Cater his heires and assignes for Ever twenty Acors of Land as an addition to his ffathers Plantation Test will ffurber Clark of sd Comitte The Above grant voated & Confermed in generalll Towne metting The sixteenth day of Aprill 1694    Test Jno Tuttle  Towne Clerk
(Dover Town Records, Vol. 3, p. 20)

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Life of Richard Cater II
SOURCE: Richard Cater-Carter of Dover, New Hampshire and Some of His Descendants, by Robert Givens (1972), This work is copyrighted - and the author gives his permission for its use at FamilySearch.org - for any other use please contact the author.
Chapter 3 The Life of Richard Cater II -
Whereas the first generation of the Cater family is fairly well documented, the second generation leaves much to be desired. The records of Richard II are meager to say the least, but it is for certain that, as Charles T. Libby believed, he did live and bear children including our next ancestor, Richard III.
As of now, the origins of Richard II are unknown though it seems likely that he was born in England about 1640 or earlier. His birth can be approximated by the fact that a Richard Cater and a Rich. Cater appear on an undated Dover voter list (“taken the oath of Fidelity”) that has been assumed to date from 1654 or 1655. (Libby, p. 49, List 355a) Assuming that Richard II must have been at least 20, his birth might be placed as early as 1635 or so.
Since his next known reference is thirty years after the previous one, and his last known record is 17 years after the second record, much of Richard II’s life has to be supposed. As was noted in the previous chapter, Richard II must have lived on his father’s property all his life because he is not mentioned in the marriage agreement of 1672 between his father and Mary Ricord. He is not mentioned in any deeds or documents concerning the Cater estate at Bloody Point after his father died in 1679 and after his step-mother’s death in 1685. The only logical reason for the land not changing hands at these times is that he was living with them and he took possession of then through reversion as the heir of Richard I. During all the time his father and step-mother lived he would not be a tax payer because the land belonged to his father first and later to his step-mother.
Richard II must have been unaffiliated with the Congregational Church as there are no church records (birth, marriage or death) pertaining to his life. Being unreligious also would have implications in other areas as well. Though his father was well established and was on local juries many times, Richard II never seems to have obtained this honor. This vein of thought can be supported by the fact that his daughter, Mary, was admitted to membership in the Congregational Church as an adult -age 20- not having been baptized as a child. (Brattle Square Church, Charlestown, Massachusetts, 1703) This seems to lend weight to the theory that Richard II was the atypical citizen of early New England.
Because of his non-church affiliation, the marriage of Richard II remains a mystery. It is not to be found in church records because he would have had to go to the civil authorities, and no record of this has been found. Possibly he either went back to England (which is highly unlikely); or, went to some other colony to look for a wife. In any case we are in the dark about his marriage except to know that the mother of his children, who was presumably his wife, was according to a deed drawn up by Richard III, buried with him (Richard II) on the old Cater estate:
Always Excepting and Reserving to the said Richard Carter Grantor, and his heires, Eighteen foot Square of Land lying in an Corner of the Orchard, where Richard Carters Grantor’ father and Mother was Burried...
(Rockingham Co. Deeds, Vol. 6, pp. 342-346.)
As far as the births of Richard II’s children are concerned, it is hard to set specific dates for any of them since no birth records for them exist.
The tracing or Richard II is also hampered as a result of 11 of the record books of Dover between 1647 and 1693 being in fragments, badly worn and with missing pages. (Scales, History of Dover, no page given.) If there were references to Richard II and his family during this period there is no hope they will ever come to light if destroyed previously.
Richard II shows up in his second document in a court case in 1685, when his cattle got loose and ate a neighbor’s cabbage. It is significant that his is listed as “Junior Cater” and not just Richard in this document.
The Desposition of Benjamin Rowlins age 23 or there about. Testified & saith that being at ye house of Mathews Nelson in the long, and heard Elizabeth Palmer Nelson’s tenant say that Caters cattle and Bickfords had spoiled all her cabbages and a great part of the town. This was spoken some time this summer by ye above said Elizabeth Palmer

3 of October 1685
Robert Mason
copied from ye original
(New Hampshire Court Records, Vol. 8, p. 421.)

Desposition of James Tobey age seventeen years or thereabouts 3 Nov 1685. Related to the above with the name Gaffon Bickford and Junior Cater.
(New Hampshire Court Records, Vol. 9, p. 23.)
It is reasonable to see that the cattle are called Richard’s since his step-mother had passed away shortly before and now he had possession of the farm.
Beginning in 1693 the name Richard Cater again begins to become more prominent. For the first time since 1679 a Richard Cater has church records, and, for the first time since 1664 there is a Richard Cater receiving or selling land. It is my opinion that the Richard in these documents is not Richard II but instead Richard III. This third Richard, who married a Margaret and their first child baptized in 1696 (New Hampshire Genealogical Record, Vol. 3, p. 54.) was not the aged father but the younger son. It seems most likely that the elder Richard II was probably a widower and possibly infirm by 1695, and by or about that time, the family farm was turned over to the children to live on and oversee. Of the children, John had died in 1691 or 1692; Mary married Edward Scheafe and had moved to his farm by 1704, so while she was yet a minor she was probably the nurse and housemaid for her father; and, Edward, who had removed to Kittery and later to Portsmouth. This leaves Richard II on the land to manage and run it. How the land was administered will be dealt with in the next chapter.
The death of Richard II occurred in the last quarter of 1702 and was noted by a Rev. John Pike in his journal. Rev. Pike was at the time a man of about 50 years of age who had lived the early part of his life in Dover and must have known Richard II as a young man.
1702, Dec 23. Old sister Downs died with Illnes, age & suffering.
Richard Cater died same day of small pox.
(New Hampshire Genealogical Record, Vol 4:99.)
The date of Dec. 23 is not to be considered the date of death, but instead the date of the entry. News traveled slowly in those days. Since on 9 Oct 1702 the children disposed of the family farm, it can be assumed that Richard II must already have been dead by that date. Remember it mentions the father of Richard the grantor as being buried on the land. Thus the obscure life of Richard II passes from us leaving more questions left unanswered than not.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Cater II於大約1640年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，他的父親Richard Carter20歲，母親Agnes Ann Richards18歲。他於大約1668年與Mary Richard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1702年12月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年63歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Cater
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the buyer of provisions for a large household, from Middle English catour, katour, kater, forms of acatour, acater ‘buyer’ (Anglo-Norman French acatour, early Old French acateor, central Old French achatour, Late Latin acceptator, an agent derivative of acceptare ‘to accept’). Modern English caterer results from the addition of a second agent suffix to the word. Compare Chater 1.
English: habitational name from Cator in Widdecombe in the Moor (Devon).
Americanized form of Dutch or German   Kater  .

variants.name: Chater
meanings: English: occupational name, corresponding to   Cater   1 but showing Parisian or central French dialect pronunciation; the two are sometimes interchangeable.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: First Recorded Slave in Portsmouth
historicEvents.description: In 1645, an African American man taken from Guinea was captured and brought to New Hampshire, he would be counted as the first African American slave in New Hampshire.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Black Heritage Trail in New Hampshire- Our African Heritage

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Cater II was born about 1640, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Carter, was 20 and his mother, Agnes Ann Richards, was 18. He married Mary Richard about 1668, in New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 23 December 1702, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 63, and was buried in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Cater II est né environ 1640, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Carter, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Agnes Ann Richards, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Mary Richard environ 1668, à New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 23 décembre 1702, à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Cater II um 1640 in Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Carter 20 und seine Mutter Agnes Ann Richards 18. Er hat um 1668 in New Hampshire, British Colonial America Mary Richard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. Dezember 1702 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America und wurde in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Cater II è nato il circa 1640 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard Carter, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Ann Richards, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Mary Richard circa 1668, nel New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 23 dicembre 1702, nel Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Cater IIが約1640年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Richard Carterは20歳，母Agnes Ann Richardsは18歳であった。約1668年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアでMary Richardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1702年12月23日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスで63歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスにて埋葬された。

ko.Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Cater II(이)가 대략 1640년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Carter(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Ann Richards(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1668년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에서 Mary Richard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1702년 12월 23일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Cater II nasceu aproximadamente 1640, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Carter, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Ann Richards, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Richard aproximadamente 1668, em New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 23 de dezembro de 1702, em Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Cater II родился около 1640 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Carter было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Ann Richards, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Richard около 1668 г. в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 23 декабря 1702 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика.
